events.text: Jeffrey Hill, 69-year-old Other male, Never smoker. Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.

Diagnosed with Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the Sigmoid colon. Clinical staging: T4aN0M0, AJCC Stage IIA. ICD-10: C18.9. Stage II colon cancer, microsatellite stable, status post hemicolectomy.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
PET/CT performed for baseline staging.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.

Underwent surgical resection: Laparoscopic left hemicolectomy with lymph node dissection (22 nodes examined, 0/22 positive). Estimated blood loss 300 mL. Length of stay 3 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: FOLFOX (5-FU/Leucovorin/Oxaliplatin) q2w x 12 cycles. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: FOLFOX (5-FU/Leucovorin/Oxaliplatin) q2w x 12 cycles. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 1 neuropathy. Dose reduction of neurotoxic agent by 25%. Gabapentin 300 mg TID started.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: FOLFOX (5-FU/Leucovorin/Oxaliplatin) q2w x 12 cycles. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 diarrhea. Managed with loperamide 4 mg then 2 mg after each loose stool. Stool studies negative.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: FOLFOX (5-FU/Leucovorin/Oxaliplatin) q2w x 12 cycles. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: FOLFOX (5-FU/Leucovorin/Oxaliplatin) q2w x 12 cycles. Cycle 5. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: FOLFOX (5-FU/Leucovorin/Oxaliplatin) q2w x 12 cycles. Cycle 6. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 6 assessment.
Developed grade 1 neuropathy. Dose reduction of neurotoxic agent by 25%. Gabapentin 300 mg TID started.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after adjuvant chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after adjuvant chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.

documents.text:
PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Hill
MRN: patient_70096e77249d
Specimen ID: S2023-40348
Date of Procedure: 2023-08-30
Date of Report: 2023-09-03

SPECIMEN: Sigmoid colon, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 4.2 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
CDX2: Positive
CK20: Positive
CK7: Negative
MLH1: Intact
MSH2: Intact
MSH6: Intact
PMS2: Intact

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Jeffrey Hill - Sigmoid colon" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 4.2 x 2.9 x 2.1 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm yellow-tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A11.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of nests of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and vesicular chromatin. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Electronically signed by: James Park, MD
Department of Pathology

PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Hill
MRN: patient_70096e77249d
Date of Study: 2023-08-30

CLINICAL INDICATION: Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Findings consistent with moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma as described.

Electronically signed by: Sophia Martinez, MD
Department of Radiology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Jeffrey Hill
MRN: patient_70096e77249d
Date: 2023-09-07
Provider: Dr. Richard Okafor

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Hill is a 69-year-old male who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon, IIA. Diagnosis made following evaluation of progressive symptoms. Stage II colon cancer, microsatellite stable, status post hemicolectomy. Pathology confirmed moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma. Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports progressive dyspnea and cough x 6 weeks.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- peripheral vascular disease
- hypertension

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Appendectomy (age 25)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Never smoker
- Alcohol: Rare alcohol use
- Occupation: Engineer
- Lives with spouse and adult children

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Sister with ovarian cancer at age 55.

ALLERGIES: Latex (contact dermatitis)

MEDICATIONS:
- Lisinopril 10 mg daily

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: mild cough, insomnia, dry mouth, intermittent nausea. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 124/84, HR 87, RR 14, Temp 98.0F, SpO2 97% on RA, Wt 95.6 kg
General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 9.3, Hgb 10.7, Plt 241, Cr 0.87

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
69-year-old male with IIA moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon. initial oncology consultation.

1. Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon, Stage IIA
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend surgical resection
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - Peripheral access adequate
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Priya Sharma, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Hill
MRN: patient_70096e77249d
Date of Report: 2023-09-12
Specimen: Sigmoid colon (FNA cell block)
Diagnosis: Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma
Panel: MSK-IMPACT (648 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 3.5 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  KRAS Wild-type - No mutation [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  APC p.R1450* (c.4348C>T) - Nonsense (Exon 16)
  TP53 p.R175H - Missense (Exon 5)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  SMAD4 p.R361C - VUS
  FBXW7 p.R465H - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - KRAS wild-type: Patient eligible for anti-EGFR therapy (cetuximab, panitumumab) if disease progresses.
  - MSS: Unlikely to benefit from single-agent immune checkpoint inhibitor.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 753x coverage.
Tumor content: 64%.

Electronically signed by: Katherine Liu, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Hill
MRN: patient_70096e77249d
Specimen ID: S2023-98482
Date of Procedure: 2023-10-02
Date of Report: 2023-10-04

SPECIMEN: Sigmoid colon, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 5.9 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.3 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
CDX2: Positive
CK20: Positive
CK7: Negative
MLH1: Intact
MSH2: Intact
MSH6: Intact
PMS2: Intact

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Jeffrey Hill - Sigmoid colon" is a tan-pink tissue fragment measuring 5.9 x 4.1 x 3.0 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm gray-white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A10.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and vesicular chromatin. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Electronically signed by: Patricia Williams, MD
Department of Pathology

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Medical Oncology

Patient: Jeffrey Hill
MRN: patient_70096e77249d
Date: 2023-10-10
Provider: Dr. Priya Sharma

CHIEF COMPLAINT: postoperative follow-up

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Hill is a 69-year-old male with IIA moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon who presents for postoperative follow-up. Patient reports mild fatigue but otherwise doing well. Appetite has improved.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: constipation, intermittent headaches, insomnia, unintentional weight loss. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 115/78, HR 79, RR 17, Temp 98.6F, SpO2 96% on RA, Wt 55.9 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 10.0, Hgb 9.7, Plt 119, Cr 1.12

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
69-year-old male with IIA moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon. postoperative follow-up.

1. Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma - postoperative follow-up
   - Treatment well tolerated, continue
   - Will check labs prior to next cycle
2. Symptom management
   - Pain well controlled on current regimen
3. Follow-up in 6 weeks

Electronically signed by: Amanda Foster, MD
Medical Oncology
